# observe_inbound_share (Data Source)

Retrieves information about an inbound Snowflake data share.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up share by name and provider account (both required for uniqueness)
data "observe_inbound_share" "customer_data" {
  share_name       = "CUSTOMER_SHARE_PROD"
  provider_account = "ACME_CORP.US-EAST-1"
}

# Look up share by ID
data "observe_inbound_share" "by_id" {
  id = "41012345"
}

# Use in table tracking
resource "observe_inbound_share_table" "events" {
  share_id      = data.observe_inbound_share.customer_data.oid
  table_name    = "CUSTOMER_EVENTS"
  schema_name   = "PUBLIC"
  dataset_label = "Customer Events"
  dataset_kind  = "Event"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `id` (String) The ID of the share to look up. Cannot be used with share_name or provider_account.
- `provider_account` (String) The Snowflake provider account (e.g., 'ACME_CORP.US-EAST-1'). Must be used with share_name.
- `share_name` (String) The Snowflake share name to look up. Must be used with provider_account.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `health` (String) The health status of the share (Healthy, Unhealthy, Unknown).
- `health_message` (String) Human-readable health status message.
- `last_health_check` (String) Timestamp of last health check.
- `oid` (String) The full OID of the share.
- `provider_type` (String) The type of share provider (e.g., 'Snowflake').
- `snowflake_provider_account` (String) Snowflake account providing the share.
- `snowflake_share_name` (String) Snowflake share name (from SnowflakeConfig).
- `status` (String) The operational state of the share (Pending, Creating, Active, Inactive, Error, Deleting).
- `table_count` (Number) Number of tracked tables in this share.
- `updated_at` (String) Last update timestamp.
